retry_reason: kref_get_unless_zero fails when the refcount is zero, meaning the object is already dead or dying; retrying cannot revive it.
contract_summary: This function performs a thread-safe lookup of a DRM mode object by ID and type, acquiring a reference (kref_get_unless_zero) on the object if it is still alive. The caller receives a pointer to the object with an incremented refcount, and must release that reference via drm_mode_object_put() when done. If the object's refcount has already dropped to zero (object is being freed), the function returns NULL — the caller must check for NULL and not use the object. The function also verifies the object's type and that the caller holds a valid lease; if these checks fail, NULL is returned without acquiring a reference. The mutex held during lookup ensures consistency.

check.reason: kref_get_unless_zero(&obj->refcount) operates on obj->refcount (a kref), with access path drm_mode_object.refcount (kref).refcount (refcount_t).refs (atomic_t).
